title: Okashi School
description: Japanese pastry & wagashi classes, taught the slow way.

sections.headline: The quiet craft of Japanese sweets
sections.dek: Small hands-on classes in wagashi and everyday okashi.
sections.body: Okashi School is a working confectionery studio. You make real sweets with your own hands — seasonal nerikiri, daifuku, dorayaki — and take them home in a paper box tied with string.

image.caption: Seasonal nerikiri on a lacquer tray

sections.title: Classes
sections.intro: Book a seat. Every class is three hours and includes tools, ingredients, and tea.

products.name: Strawberry Daifuku
products.blurb: Pound mochi and wrap sweet beans.

sections.title: About the studio
columns: We are a two-room studio near the river: one room for pounding mochi and simmering bean paste, one quiet room with a long cedar table where the shaping happens. Classes are capped at eight.
Our teacher trained for nine years in a Kyoto wagashi house before opening the school. The recipes are traditional; the pace is gentle; English and Japanese are both spoken.

sections.title: Visit
sections.body: Okashi School · 3-12 Kawabata-dori · open Wednesday to Sunday, 10:00–17:00 · a five-minute walk from the station.
